problem.summary: Compare one-block-per-task scheduling against a persistent CTA work queue for irregular task durations.
outputs: output[task_id] contains a deterministic per-task reduction
constraints: Persistent kernel must bound resident CTAs explicitly
Atomic queue overhead can be a negative example for uniform tasks
Record occupancy and launch configuration before claiming a scheduling win

verification.method: Compare output per task against the one-block-per-task baseline.

curation.notes: Use this to preserve both persistent-kernel wins and negative examples where queue overhead dominates.
